sourcePolicy: Declared surface: official-current > mcp-runtime > historical. Observed reliability: current live positive and negative evidence governs; conflicts require mixed or blocked.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after app update, device change, endpoint/token reset, MCP protocol change, or before any stateful phone matrix.
claims.notes: ADB device/app/PID checks, five MCP top-level reads, all 17 selected runtime documents, and a read-only tavo_status tools/call passed. The gate does not prove new 0.92 hook, TTS, ASR, theme, backup, or WebView semantics.

claims.staleness_policy: Promote individual entry/config/chat/message/input/generation/TTS claims only after matching 0.92 phone assertions pass.
claims.notes: Official and runtime docs declare entry precedence, hook-only entry support, config.get/all, chat/message notifications, input interception, generation lifecycle hooks, input.send results, and plugin TTS. These are declared surfaces, not live reliability claims.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after plugin loader, manifest, config, WebView runtime, or app-version changes.
claims.notes: F01-F04 proved entry-only execution without UI contributions, legacy execution, entry precedence over legacy, effective config defaults/overrides, and all() copy isolation.

claims.staleness_policy: Retest chat alias delivery after plugin runtime, chat lifecycle, WebView, or app changes.
claims.notes: Specific message ordering, persistent-add boundary, and handler isolation passed; a controlled chat update did not reach the chat:changed compatibility handler as chat:updated.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after input pipeline, plugin runtime, WebView, MCP send, or app changes.
claims.notes: F06/F10 proved UI, TavoJS, and MCP rewrite/cancel/fail-open plus structured acceptance-time send results. Attachment preservation was not exposed and busy was not observed.

claims.staleness_policy: Retest every generation source and auxiliary exclusion after generation pipeline or app changes.
claims.notes: Prepare, success, fail-open, sanitized errors, cancellation persistence, and terminal exclusivity passed. The controlled othersContinuation path did not emit a Hook or message; auxiliary exclusions remained blocked.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after package importer, update, backup schema, restore flow, or app-version changes.
claims.notes: Root, single-wrapper, and development-zip imports passed; same-id update preserved config/enabled/runtime; native Backup B restored id/version/config/enabled/runtime. Only absolute/traversal negatives were live-rejected; remaining negative fixtures are offline-only.

claims.staleness_policy: Retest after TTS provider, binding, queue, audio backend, plugin runtime, or app changes.
claims.notes: Configured character and queued fake-gateway requests were accepted; persona was not retested with a working binding. Identity, quality, and audible stop semantics remain manual.

claims.staleness_policy: Retest after native ASR UI, permission, provider protocol, composer, or app changes.
claims.notes: Docs/MCP do not expose ASR, but native UI and multipart fake-gateway transcription worked with release-to-send, cancel, fill, edit, clear, and bounded transport failure. Human recognition accuracy remains manual.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after theme editor, voice-rule UI, settings persistence, or app changes.
claims.notes: Bold/italic, bubble sides, 30/70 percent visual-novel previews, and role/user voice-rule scope persisted in UI; original settings were restored. No audible filtering claim is made.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after image provider form, generation protocol, chat insertion, or app changes.
claims.notes: A one-pixel fake-gateway response proved image invocation/insertion only. Disposable NovelAI save/reopen/delete proved UI lifecycle only; no visual quality or real-provider wire claim is made.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after Tavo app update, token reset, endpoint change, or MCP transport change.
claims.notes: Direct tool-name JSON-RPC methods failed; call Tavo tools with JSON-RPC method tools/call.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun before using as proof for a new app version or new phone.
claims.notes: tavo_input_set/get/send plus message count/find/get proved a short message entered the real chat.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun if Android version, device, accessibility state, or Tavo UI framework changes.
claims.notes: Accessibility service was not enabled; UIAutomator bounds plus adb input tap focused the chat input.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun before claiming exact worldbook field preservation.
claims.notes: Import accepted a disposable lorebook but readback normalized the entry, so import acceptance is not field preservation.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun when validating character import, chat creation, or current-chat switching.
claims.notes: Disposable card import, chat creation, current chat switch, greeting selector confirmation, and MCP/UI readback worked.

claims.staleness_policy: Must run Android screenshot/click/readback proof before answering as verified.
claims.notes: Use Advanced Rendering or plugin path; exact CSS positioning and click behavior are not current-product facts yet.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun before any phone validation session or after device/app/UI state changes.
claims.notes: ADB device/package/window/UIAutomator/screenshot capture passed on real phone; validation files retained.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun before quoting current tool/resource counts or using MCP tools.
claims.notes: Strict MCP initialize/tools/resources/templates/prompts discovery passed: 70 tools, 18 resources, 7 templates, 0 prompts.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after plugin manifest/runtime changes or Tavo app update.
claims.notes: Minimal official manifest.json plugin was validated, packaged, dry-run installed, actually installed, read back, and runtime contributions exposed inputActions/htmlFragments.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after AR/JS setting changes, plugin runtime changes, or app update.
claims.notes: Input + menu showed Codex Fixture Ping, first click required Advanced Rendering, after enabling AR and JavaScript auto the action appended marker into input; MCP input_get read it back.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after app, model endpoint, semantic runner, fixture, AR/JS setting, or plugin runtime changes.
claims.notes: Terminal v23 passed 50/50 primary calls across ten families plus 5/5 controls. All 55 exchanges had unique persistent user and assistant message IDs. The separate 17-action UI preflight sent zero model requests and does not count toward this KPI. v23 reverified the earlier 50 imported assets; it did not import another 50 files.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after character prompt assembly, preset marker, app, or model endpoint changes.
claims.notes: Five imported CCv2 characters exposed unique markers found only in data.description to five real model replies. This proves the tested description path, not greeting selection, alternate greeting materialization, or mes_example placement.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after lorebook activation, preset marker, app, or model endpoint changes.
claims.notes: Five independent native constant entries using lorebookAfter/system, probability 100, and zero sticky/cooldown/delay produced their unique facts in 5/5 real replies. Bound-decoy and unbound controls also passed. The v23 primary assertions did not test the keyword probe entries stored in the same books.

claims.staleness_policy: Always rerun a bound positive and unbound negative in the target chat before relying on keyword activation.
claims.notes: A single-keyword native entry with secondary strategy none produced its unique fact through both MCP-switch and UI-switch diagnostic branches. This is a dated positive observation, not a reliability guarantee.

claims.staleness_policy: Treat keyword activation as mixed on 0.91.0 and rerun target-chat positive and negative controls after runtime changes.
claims.notes: After app/MCP restart, v20 recorded 15 failed keyword attempts while constant controls continued to inject. History, input-settle, post-restart constant, and constant-plus-keyword diagnostics narrowed the reproducible regression to keyword activation. Together with the same-day positive claim, the product verdict is mixed. v23 did not retest keyword as an asserted outcome.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after regex pipeline, app, model endpoint, or fixture changes.
claims.notes: Bound char placement, editAndReceive timing, substitution none, and null depth persisted CLEANED markers in 5/5 assistant messages; one unbound control retained RAW. This does not cover Markdown/code-block safety or other placements, timings, substitutions, and depths.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after preset assembly, active-preset behavior, app, or model endpoint changes.
claims.notes: Five single relative/system custom entries enforced a unique reply prefix and OBSERVE, QUESTION, VERIFY output order; one unbound control omitted the marker and active preset 2 was restored. This does not prove multi-entry prompt position, absolute depth, or provider wire roles.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after persona prompt assembly, chat binding, app, or model endpoint changes.
claims.notes: Five bound persona descriptions supplied unique identity markers and role text to real replies; one unbound control omitted its marker. This does not prove persona export/import preservation.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after EJS, macro, TavoJS, plugin runtime, app, or model endpoint changes.
claims.notes: Five fresh plugin-generated chat-scope tokens were read by EJS, and five EJS incvar effects were read back by plugin TavoJS with counter delta +1. The cases also covered EJS set/get, default, condition, loop, and EJS-produced char/user macros. Global/message scope, general macro-variable interoperability, and mixed escaping remain unverified.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after WebView, AR/JS setting, viewport, app, or panel source changes.
claims.notes: Rendered message panels were visible and clickable; chat-scope tavo.set/get returned clicked, tavo.input.set/append changed the composer, and screenshots plus UI XML plus MCP input readback agreed. Coverage is one phone viewport and does not prove restart persistence, sanitization, multi-viewport layout, or global/message scope.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after plugin manifest/actions, AR setting, plugin runtime, or app changes.
claims.notes: Five native inputActions executed through the plugin action menu, used chat-scope set/get plus input set/append, and fed five real model calls. Markers were TPG_<run>_* from actions.js; this does not prove clicks inside the HTML fragment, whose source markers are TPG_PANEL_<run>_*.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after MCP message schema, context assembly, app, or model endpoint changes.
claims.notes: Five assistant messages were directly appended, read by stable ID, updated, read back with the updated content, and then observed by five real model replies while the original marker stayed forbidden. MCP insert and delete effects, and TavoJS/TPG message CRUD, remain unverified.

claims.staleness_policy: Rebuild after any source artifact, selection contract, runner, app, MCP identity, or model endpoint change.
claims.notes: Coverage index for 35 canonical cases: 19 complete model-exchange passes, 15 complete model-exchange assertion failures, and one partial direct-runtime case without a final exchange. It contains 68 unique persistent exchange message IDs and all selected source runs restored state. It is not one green epoch and does not count toward the KPI.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun paired isolated positives and negatives after worldbook runtime, app, preset marker, or model endpoint changes.
claims.notes: Constant and four marker-position observations returned isolated markers, while keyword, andAny positive, in-window, several lifecycle positives, and atDepth/system did not. Negative observations and cooldown-expired were mixed. Lifecycle cases include entry updates and MCP history mutations, so they are combination observations rather than field-independent semantics.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after greeting picker, character schema, preset dialogueExamples marker, app, or model changes.
claims.notes: Main, alternate A, and alternate B target markers were each found in a persistent assistant message by stable ID. Selection artifacts do not prove index zero or exclude other greeting text. An example-only marker reached one real model reply but does not prove exact prompt position, role, or START handling.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after preset assembly, history trimming, app, or model endpoint changes.
claims.notes: Single absolute entries configured at depth 0 and depth 3 each supplied an isolated marker to a complete real model exchange. Marker visibility does not prove exact adjacent prompt position, multi-entry order, or provider wire role.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after composer, input tool, app, or normal-send flow changes.
claims.notes: Clear, empty readback, set, append, exact full composer readback, and normal send completed with stable user and assistant IDs. Current input_append inserts one ASCII separator between existing and appended text, so split at an existing target space and always verify input_get.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after message schema, revision, delete policy, app, or context assembly changes.
claims.notes: One stable message ID completed append/read/update/read and retained-tombstone readback; delete only passed dry-run and actual delete was intentionally not executed. The later model saw the retained marker but failed the nonce-first formatting assertion, which does not invalidate direct CRU proof.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after plugin scoped facade, TavoJS lorebook API, confirmation UI, or app changes.
claims.notes: Plugin-scoped TavoJS create/read/update and retained tombstone update completed for worldbook id 315 and MCP readback matched markers. Create showed a create confirmation and updates showed modify confirmations in this run. Actual delete was not executed and message-panel TavoJS is a separate host.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after current-chat navigation, MCP host, plugin runtime, app restart, or endpoint identity changes.
claims.notes: Five plugin actions wrote/read distinct chat-scope values across chats A and B. A final current_chat_set returned success and a target diff, but immediate plus nine polled readbacks remained on B, so the sequence terminated before the sixth action or model exchange. This is a strong success-without-effect runtime signal, not a completed full-case verdict.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun restoration verification after every stateful semantic epoch.
claims.notes: The epoch restored the original private chat, active preset set [2], and all 58 plugin enabled states; final plugin runtime contributions matched the pre-run hash.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after EJS engine, macro engine, prompt assembly, provider adapter, character field mapping, or app version changes.
claims.notes: A normal phone send produced a captured OpenAI-compatible request ordered system, prior user, prior assistant, current user. Character-description set/get/default, condition, loop, increment, and EJS-produced character/user macros were fully rendered in system content; raw EJS and char/user macro tags were absent. The persistent test exchange completed with ACK. This proves the tested character-description path, not every EJS-capable field.

claims.staleness_policy: Refresh after the official JavaScript API docs or tavo.generate contract changes.
claims.notes: context defaults to false; context true includes current conversation state. preset and settings are optional per-call overrides and should be omitted when preserving the active chat configuration.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun the same-PID multi-round FD gate after an app, Android WebView, plugin host, or bridge change.
claims.notes: Background polling of tavo.chat.current and tavo.input.get showed cumulative FD growth on Android. Do not generalize this scoped regression to every return-valued or every TavoJS API.

claims.staleness_policy: Rerun after Android WebView, plugin runtime, injected fetch implementation, or Tavo app changes.
claims.notes: Invoking a stored bare window.fetch with the wrong receiver raised Illegal invocation. Bind the WebView-native method to globalThis and keep a receiver-checking test; Node or jsdom mocks alone are insufficient.
